topic: A person discovers their partner has been taking secret phone calls
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment had become a heavy, suffocating blanket. I watched Mark across the living room, his attention constantly snagged by the small, insistent vibration of his phone. It happened again: a brief, sharp intake of breath, a quick glance down, and then the sudden, almost practiced lowering of the device. I didn't say anything. The words felt lodged somewhere behind my teeth, too heavy to form. My shoulders slumped further, a physical manifestation of the draining fatigue that had settled deep in my bones all week. I just started tracing patterns on the condensation of my glass, the ice melting slowly, mirroring the slow erosion of my own patience.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked beneath a cushion on the nightstand—a pattern of late-night calls, timestamps that didn't align with any shared activity. My hands shook slightly as I picked up the phone, the screen illuminating a conversation that was clearly not meant for my eyes. A dull ache pulsed behind my eyes. I didn't confront him immediately. Instead, I retreated to the kitchen, the clatter of dishes sounding deafeningly loud. I stared at the sink, the water running cold over my hands, feeling a profound sense of emptiness. Every muscle in my body felt loose, uncooperative, as if I were running on fumes.

[story 3]

It was the way he moved, the sudden rigidity in his posture when the phone buzzed—that was what truly got to me. I saw the flicker of something private in his eyes, a quick shift in focus that suggested a world existing just beyond our shared space. I didn't want to start a fight; the energy required felt monumental. I simply stood by the doorway, letting the quiet observation settle. My jaw ached from clenching it so tightly. I felt profoundly drained, like a battery that had run completely dry, and all I could manage was a slow, deliberate turn away, seeking a quiet corner where I could just… stop moving.
